_comment: Wiring map for the required-status-check drift watcher (see .github/scripts/required_check_drift.py). Each entry in 'checks' is a security-load-bearing merge block: 'required_context' is the EXACT status-check context string that must stay listed as a required check on each repo's default branch, and 'repos' lists the repos where it was wired. A reusable workflow called via 'uses:' emits its check as '<caller-job-id> / <reusable-job-name>' (NOT the workflow display name), so the contexts here are those exact emitted strings. 'mechanism' records HOW each requirement was originally set (ruleset = a branch ruleset's required_status_checks rule; classic = branches/<default>/protection/required_status_checks) and is informational only — the checker passes a repo if the context is required via EITHER mechanism, so an intentional ruleset rename or a mechanism swap does not false-alarm. The 'lockfile-registry' check (Task #308) guards the six repos that commit lockfiles; the 'overclaim-honesty' check (Task #393) guards the two repos where the doc-honesty guard is a required merge check. Re-add a repo under the right check if it later gets the requirement wired.

checks.required_context: lockfiles / No lockfile references a Replit-internal registry host
overclaim / Governed surfaces are honest (Theorem U citation rule)
